findings.problem: Several pivotal paragraphs reuse the same corrective sentence architecture ('There it was', 'It was not ...'), so different turns in the hearing land with a similar authored cadence instead of scene-specific pressure. At shortlist standard, those beats need more variation in syntax and image logic.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the beat-openers at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:39, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:121, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:167, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:215, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:299, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:315 so they do not all pivot through the same corrective formula. Keep at most the single strongest instance, and re-render the others through concrete courtroom detail, bodily sensation, or wage-loss consequence specific to that moment.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:39, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:121, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:167, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:215, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:299, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:315 no longer repeat the same 'There it was' / 'It was not ...' turn more than once across the set, and each cited beat is distinguishable on the page by a different concrete image or bodily consequence. Fail if multiple cited beats still rely on the same corrective template.

summary: Chapter 12 lands the required bureaucratic ordeal: Yolanda's exposure is materially legible, Mira's testimony is specific enough to force a stay, the ending arrives as expulsion rather than applause, and character voices remain consistent with the style bible's register/contraction guidance. No continuity or narrative-tense contradictions surfaced in the allowed context. The unresolved problem is sentence-level repetition in several major beats, which flattens the prose enough to block a clean prose pass.
